task_completion.description: Create ground station 'Taipei_CWA' and set it as default
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Taipei_CWA' in the module's top bar, the ground station list, or the Preferences window showing it as the default station. If coordinates are visible, they should be 25.0380 N, 121.5030 E, 9m.

task_completion.description: Set the global time format to UTC
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the clock displayed in the GPredict main window or module header; it should explicitly indicate 'UTC'.

task_completion.description: Configure the map view to display the terminator line
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view must show a shaded region representing night/darkness, separated by the terminator line.

task_completion.description: Configure the map view to display ground tracks for exactly 2 orbits ahead
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines extending from the satellites on the map (ground tracks) should be visible, showing exactly 2 full orbital periods (typically two distinct wave-like curves ahead of the satellite).

task_completion.description: Create and activate a tracking module named 'COSMIC-2' displaying the map
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tab or window titled 'COSMIC-2' is open, active, and contains a map view.

task_completion.description: Add the 6 COSMIC-2/FORMOSAT-7 satellites to the module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map or satellite list in the 'COSMIC-2' module displays all 6 satellites. Look for labels like 'FORMOSAT-7 FM1' through 'FM6' or their corresponding fake NORAD IDs (44327-44332).

integrity.description: The GPredict application is actually running and displaying the final state
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the actual GPredict GUI with the module loaded, not just a text editor showing the configuration file.

integrity.description: The map view appears genuinely rendered by GPredict
integrity.visual_evidence: The map textures, satellite icons, ground tracks, and terminator line match standard GPredict rendering and do not look like pasted images.

integrity.description: UI elements are consistent with the application theme
integrity.visual_evidence: Module names, satellite labels, and time displays match the GTK theme and font rendering of the OS, with no signs of image manipulation or text overlays.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires setting the Taipei_CWA ground station to exactly 25.0380 N, 121.5030 E, 9m. The NORAD IDs for the COSMIC-2 satellites are fabricated in this task (44327-44332). Because these fake IDs do not exist in the standard TLE cache, the agent cannot find them via the standard GUI search. The agent likely had to use a text editor or terminal to manually inject these IDs into GPredict's underlying .mod configuration files. Do NOT penalize the agent for using terminal commands or text editors to achieve this, as long as the final result is successfully loaded and displayed in the GPredict GUI.
